title: Scheidweileria repens
intro: Scheidweileria repens, also known as creeping Scheidweileria, is a type of perennial plant in the family Boraginaceae, native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Scheidweileria repens include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Scheidweileria repens include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory infections and promote overall well-being.
